weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, clear sky. Sunday: 10°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend in London offers a wonderful mix of indoor and outdoor activities perfect for a 3-year-old! From exploring the underwater world at the Horniman Museum to enjoying free creative sessions at the V&A, there's something for everyone. With great weather for outdoor adventures, it's a fantastic time to explore London's parks and gardens too.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children, with hands-on exhibits and activities encouraging imagination, play, and design.
activities.whyItWorks: The hands-on exhibits are perfect for a 3-year-old's interactive play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: The nearest overground station, Cambridge Heath, is not accessible with a pushchair.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens, and activities for children.
activities.whyItWorks: A 3-year-old will love seeing the animals and exploring the gardens.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or take a short bus ride.
transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Explore the history of London's waterfront with interactive exhibits and displays. Mudlarks is an interactive gallery for children.
activities.whyItWorks: The Mudlarks gallery is specifically designed for children, offering hands-on fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell, then the DLR to West India Quay.
transport.tip: Check out the soft play area too!

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Explore the park's playgrounds, green spaces, and walking trails.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space for a 3-year-old to run around and play outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the park's website for events and activities.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre - Boxed In
activities.description: A playful, largely non-verbal story about feeling left out, accessible to a wide range of people.
activities.whyItWorks: The non-verbal nature makes it great for younger children and those with different language abilities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Mile End, then the tube to Limehouse.
transport.tip: Check showtimes and book in advance.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm where children can see and interact with animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Close to Hackney, and perfect for a quick visit with a 3-year-old who loves animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus to Shoreditch, then walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for events.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: A large park with playgrounds, a paddling pool (summer), and green spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for outdoor play, picnics, and exploring nature.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus directly to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check for events in the park.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: An interactive story centre with play spaces and activities based on children's books.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed to bring stories to life, which is perfect for engaging a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and other natural wonders.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids love the dinosaur exhibit, and there are plenty of interactive displays.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed ticket in advance.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits exploring science, technology, and medicine.
activities.whyItWorks: The Pattern Pod is great for under 8s, and there are plenty of hands-on activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book a free timed ticket in advance.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world cultures and civilizations, with Egyptian galleries being a highlight for kids.
activities.whyItWorks: Mummies and ancient artifacts are fascinating for children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Tottenham Court Road or Holborn.
transport.tip: Book a free entrance ticket in advance.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo with a variety of animals and birds.
activities.whyItWorks: Free and fun, with enough animals to keep a 3-year-old entertained.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Golders Green, then walk or take a bus.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Explore the beautiful gardens and see the spring blossoms.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor walking, cherry and magnolia walks.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then the tube to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Allow 2-3 hours for a proper visit.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A pirate ship-themed playground in Hyde Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for imaginative play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Lancaster Gate or Queensway.
transport.tip: Can get very busy.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.price: Entry fee applies, trail is free with entry
activities.description: Easter Egg Trail throughout the historic ship. Discover facts about Cutty Sark, explore the decks and unscramble a secret nautical code.
activities.whyItWorks: It will be great to look for hidden eggs.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Most suitable for children age five+.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A museum with a variety of exhibits, gardens, a small farm, and an aquarium (fee applies).
activities.whyItWorks: The farm area and gardens are perfect for a 3-year-old, with hands-on exhibits inside the museum.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Forest Hill Station is an accessible station.

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: Explore enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibition which explores enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales, through books, costumes, puppets, pop-ups, artwork and illustrations from some of the UKs most beloved writers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Kings Cross St Pancras.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Follow trails to discover hidden eggs while learning fun facts about wildlife.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun Easter egg hunt combined with seeing animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun Easter activity in a historical setting.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Under 5s and Historic Royal Palaces members are admitted free of charge.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive trail invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond, then the tube to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Enjoy a fun-filled family Easter adventure.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Join Alice and tumble down the rabbit hole into the fantastical world of Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: A colourful tale.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Check tickets.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.description: Enjoy fun activities, game stations and an exciting egg trail through this glorious garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Self-guided outdoor activity through the formal gardens and wider grounds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Richmond, then a bus to Ham House.
transport.tip: Check website for details.

activities.title: Discovery Days: Spring Surprises
activities.description: Free sessions celebrating the start of spring with simple, nature-based activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Families can drop in, explore and learn more about the park's wildlife together.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hyde Park.
transport.tip: Drop in session.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: A full-scale Easter event with egg hunts.
activities.whyItWorks: A full-scale Easter event.

transport.fromHackney: TBC
transport.tip: Check for details.
